title: relay.rs: add() does not validate inbox_uri scheme before fetch
description: In relay::add, the relay actor document is fetched and inbox_uri is extracted from actor["inbox"]. The SSRF guard checks that inbox_uri does not resolve to a private host, but there is no check that inbox_uri starts with https://. A malicious relay actor document could return an inbox like http://169.254.169.254/..., file:///etc/passwd, or ftp://.... The http:// case passes the existing guard because is_private_host_resolved uses port 443 for DNS lookup but the actual delivery request would use port 80. The None branch of host_str() (e.g. file://) skips the guard entirely. Fix: add an explicit starts_with("https://") check on inbox_uri before the SSRF guard, consistent with how inbox URIs are validated in process_batch (delivery.rs line 167).
close_reason: Fixed: relay inbox_uri must start with https://.
